info.name: Movate — Core Flow
info.description:
A top-to-bottom live demo of the mdk (`movate-cli`) runtime `/api/v1` surface against a deployed Azure Container Apps runtime.

Each request maps to an `mdk <verb>` (or is labelled **API-first** when there is no CLI equivalent) and demonstrates one core feature. Run the folders in order — `test` scripts capture key IDs (`agent_name`, `run_id`, `session_id`, `project_id`, `graph_node_id`) into collection/environment variables so the chain flows without manual copy/paste.

**Auth:** collection-level Bearer using `{{bearer_token}}` (set it in the environment — never hardcoded). The token needs `admin` + `read` (and `run` + `kb:write` for the run/KB steps). See `postman/README.md` for how to mint one with `mdk auth create-key`.

**Entry point:** start with `GET /api/v1/capabilities` to discover what the runtime supports before running the rest of the flow.

**Source of truth:** request bodies and scopes mirror `src/movate/runtime/app.py` + `src/movate/runtime/schemas.py` and the audit in `docs/front-end-api.md`. You can also `Import → Link → {{runtime_url}}/api/v1/openapi.json` for the full typed API.

item.name: Capabilities
item.description: API-first entry point — call this first to discover what the deployed runtime supports. No CLI equivalent; use this before anything else to check which models, feature flags, and optional extras are active.

request.description:
CLI equivalent: none (API-first). Use this first to see what's enabled.

Returns a capability matrix — reachable models, feature flags (detected from the live route table / importable modules), the scope vocabulary, this tenant's effective limits, and the installed optional extras.

Auth is optional: a missing/bad bearer returns a minimal subset (`mdk_version` + `api_version`, `minimal: true`) for version-fingerprint use. A valid `read`-scoped bearer returns the full matrix. BYOK provider names are listed but the key values are never surfaced.

Scope: none required (minimal); `read` for full matrix.

item.name: Movate — Core Flow
item.description: The ordered demo. Run from top to bottom (Postman Runner or one-by-one).

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk auth whoami`.

Returns the calling key's `key_id`, `tenant_id`, `env`, `scopes`, and `expires_at`. Use it to confirm the token is valid and carries `admin` + `read` (the demo also exercises `run` and `kb:write`) before running the rest of the flow. No scope gate — only authentication is required.

item.name: 1a. Create agent (from wizard JSON) — mdk init

body.raw:
{
  "name": "{{agent_name}}",
  "agent_provider": "Movate",
  "agent_type": "Task Agent",
  "role": "Assistant",
  "description": "An FAQ assistant. Answers questions concisely with a confidence score.",
  "agent_role": "Concise, friendly support agent; cites sources when grounded.",
  "agent_goal": "Answer product FAQs accurately and briefly.",
  "agent_prompt": "You are an FAQ assistant. Answer the user's question concisely using the provided knowledge base context. If you are unsure, say so.\n\nQuestion: {{question}}",
  "reference_output": "Our standard plan includes 5 seats and 100GB storage.",
  "mcp_connectors": ["web-search"],
  "knowledge_store": ["product-docs"],
  "ai_model": "openai/gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18",
  "ai_foundation": "Azure"
}

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk init` (agent-authoring half).

Create an agent in the runtime registry from the Mova iO "Onboard Agent" wizard JSON (`WizardAgentSubmission`). This is the simplest live create path: structured fields → canonical `agent.yaml` + `prompt.md`, no file upload.

`mcp_connectors` → the agent's `skills` and `knowledge_store` → the agent's `contexts`, so this single request demonstrates skills + contexts wiring. **Both must already exist on the runtime** or the bundle 422s on `load_agent()`: register the skill first via request `4` (`POST /skills`) and ensure the named context exists (or remove those two arrays for a no-dependency create). Scope: `admin`. Returns `AgentCreatedView` (`name`, `version`, `agent_dir`, ...).

Note on the CLI caveat: `mdk init` on disk *also* scaffolds a local project — that filesystem half is CLI/ops-only. This endpoint is the runtime "make this agent exist on the server" half.

exec: pm.test('201 Created (or 409 if it already exists)', () => [201, 409].includes(pm.response.code));

formdata.description: OPTION A — a single .zip of the canonical layout (agent.yaml, prompt.md, schema/, optionally skills/<name>/, contexts/<name>.md, kb/). Mutually exclusive with the individual-file fields below; supply EITHER bundle OR the four files, not both.
OPTION B — agent.yaml (required in individual-files mode).
OPTION B — prompt.md (required in individual-files mode).
OPTION B — schema/input.json (required in individual-files mode).
OPTION B — schema/output.json (required in individual-files mode).
OPTIONAL, repeating — each is a contexts/<name>.md the agent declares in `contexts:`.
OPTIONAL, repeating — each is a kb/<name>.json corpus file.

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk init` / `mdk deploy --mode agents`.

The bundle/multipart variant — `POST /api/v1/agents`. Use this when you already have a pre-built canonical bundle (e.g. produced by `mdk init --llm` locally, since there is NO server-side LLM authoring endpoint — see `docs/front-end-api.md`).

Two mutually-exclusive modes: attach a single `bundle` .zip (Option A, enabled below), OR enable the four individual files `agent_yaml` + `prompt` + `input_schema` + `output_schema` (Option B). A bundle that ships `skills/<name>/` folders persists those skills into the registry first, then the agent — so the full skill + context wiring can ride in one upload. Scope: `admin`. Returns `AgentCreatedView`.

This request is illustrative — attach your own file(s) before sending. Disabled by default in the auto-run is the individual-file set; pick one mode.

CLI equivalent: `mdk edit <agent>` (re-deploy).

Update a deployed agent by re-uploading its canonical bundle. PUT is a MULTIPART bundle (either a single zipped `bundle` part, or the four individual files: `agent.yaml` + `prompt.md` + `schema/input.json` + `schema/output.json`) — NOT a JSON patch. A content-changed PUT writes a new immutable registry version in Postgres (ADR 014/021); an unchanged re-upload is a no-op. Scope: `admin`.

CLI equivalent: `mdk validate`.

Run the prompt linter + cost forecast against the already-stored agent. No request body. Returns `AgentValidationView` (`passed`, `errors[]`, `warnings[]`, `cost_forecast`). This is the soft "is it shippable?" gate; the hard structural validation already ran at create time (a malformed bundle 422s before it ever lands). Scope: `read` (read-only despite being a POST).

formdata.description: One or more documents to ingest. Repeat the `files` key for multiple uploads. Supported: .md / .markdown / .txt / .pdf / .docx / .html / .htm. Attach a small doc (e.g. a product-FAQ.md) before sending.

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk kb ingest`.

Upload one or more documents into the agent's knowledge base. `multipart/form-data` with a repeating `files` field; each doc is chunked, embedded, and persisted (deduped on content hash). Returns `KbIngestView` (`files[]` with per-file `status`, `total_chunks_saved`). Scope: `kb:write`.

Note: this endpoint takes uploaded files only — there is no inline-text or URL body field. To ingest a URL, fetch it to an .html/.md file first and upload that. Attach your own small doc before sending (this request ships no file).

CLI equivalent: `mdk kb stats`.

Server-side aggregate of the agent's KB. Returns `KbStatsView` (`total_chunks`, `total_chars`, `ocr_chunks`, per-`sources` breakdown, distinct embedding `models`). Confirms the ingest from 3a actually landed. Scope: `read`.

CLI equivalent: `mdk kb search`.

Semantic search over the agent's KB (`KbSearchSubmission`: `question`, `k`, `hybrid`). Set `hybrid: true` to fuse vector + BM25 lexical results. Returns `KbSearchView` with scored `results[]`. Shows the retrieval that grounds the run in step 6. Scope: `read`.

formdata.description: REQUIRED — the skill.yaml spec. Its `name:` field decides the on-disk skill directory and is the name an agent references in `skills: [...]`.
OPTIONAL — corpus.json shipped alongside the skill.

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk skills add`.

Create or replace a skill bundle in the runtime's skill registry (PUT semantics: re-uploading the same name overwrites). `multipart/form-data`: `skill_yaml` (required) + optional `impl` / `corpus` / `readme`. Returns `SkillCreatedView`. Scope: `admin`.

Run this BEFORE request 1a if your wizard body references this skill in `mcp_connectors` — an agent declaring `skills: [<name>]` 422s if the skill isn't already registered. Attach your own skill.yaml before sending.

CLI equivalent: `mdk deploy --mode agents`.

Push the agent's canonical bundle to the per-tenant GitHub repo as one commit (ADR 007). `AgentPublishSubmission`: all fields optional (`commit_message`, `author_name`, `author_email`). Returns `AgentPublishedView` (commit SHA + URL). Scope: `admin`.

Requires `MDK_GITHUB_ENABLED=1` + GitHub App config on the runtime, else 503 (the route is still advertised). CLI caveat: this is the *agent promotion* sense of deploy — the *infra* sense (build the runtime image, roll Azure Container Apps) stays CLI/ops-only via `mdk deploy --mode runtime`.

exec: pm.test('200, or 503 when GitHub publishing is disabled on the runtime', () => [200, 503].includes(pm.response.code));
else { console.log('Publish disabled (MDK_GITHUB_ENABLED off) — expected in many demo runtimes.'); }

query.description: Inline (synchronous) mode — execute in-process and return the RunView (200). Omit or set false to queue an async job (202 + job_id) and poll GET /api/v1/jobs/{job_id}.

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk run <agent>`.

Submit an agent run. `AgentRunSubmission`: `input` (matches the agent's input schema — here `{question}`), optional `mock`, `notify_email`, `thread_id`, `session_id`. With `?wait=true` the runtime executes synchronously and returns a `RunView` with the grounded answer (200). Default (`wait=false`) queues a job and returns 202 + `{job_id}`. Scope: `run`.

The `test` script captures `run_id` into a collection/environment variable so the monitor steps (7a/7b) can fetch it. Tip: add `"mock": true` to the body to run against the deterministic MockProvider (no API key, sub-second) for a guaranteed-fast demo.

To run within a session, add `"session_id": "{{session_id}}"` to the body — the runtime loads prior turns as context and appends the new turn (ADR 045 D10). Session must already exist (see Stateful Sessions folder).

item.name: 7a. Monitor — get run — mdk runs get

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk runs get`.

Fetch a single run including its `output`, status, timing, and cost. Returns `RunView`. Uses the `{{run_id}}` captured in step 6. Scope: `read`.

item.name: 7b. Monitor — run trace — mdk runs trace

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk runs trace`.

The reconstructed span tree for the run (prompt, model calls, tool/skill invocations, retrieval). Returns `RunTraceView`. This is the observability payoff of the demo. Scope: `read`.

query.description: OPTIONAL — only count runs / evals from the last N days. 0 (default) = all-time. Mirrors `mdk report --last N`. Range 0–3650.
OPTIONAL — how many failing cases to surface in `top_failing_cases` (default 5). Range 1–50.

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk report`.

The cross-agent aggregate monitor feed (ADR 032 D2). Returns `ReportView`: tenant-scoped pass-rate trends, cost-over-time, latency p50/p95/p99, top failing cases, and a per-agent/workflow rollup over the requested `window`. This is the fleet-wide "how are my agents doing?" panel the Mova iO front end renders — the aggregate complement to the per-run fetch + trace (7a/7b). Tenant-scoped; an empty store returns a zeroed report (200), never a 500. Scope: `read`.

Optional query params (disabled by default — enable to scope the rollup): `?window=<days>` narrows to the last N days (0 = all-time) and `?top=<n>` sets how many failing cases to surface. For a single agent's slice use `GET /api/v1/agents/{name}/metrics` (same `read` scope, same params).

item.name: Async Eval (on Azure)
item.description: The asynchronous eval lifecycle — exactly how it runs against a DEPLOYED Azure runtime (Postgres-backed jobs, separate worker pod). Kick off → 202 + job_id → poll the job until terminal → fetch the scorecard. This is the same async pattern every long-running verb (runs, llm-create, eval) uses on Azure; nothing here needs the request to block.

item.name: E1. Kick off eval (async) — mdk eval

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk eval <agent>`.

`EvalSubmission`: omit `wait` (or `wait:false`) for the ASYNC path — the runtime creates a `JobRecord(kind=EVAL)` in Postgres and returns **202 `{job_id, status:"queued"}`** immediately; the worker pod claims + executes it. (`wait:true` runs inline and returns the scorecard directly — convenient for a single-process demo, but avoid for big datasets.) `mock:true` skips real provider calls so the lifecycle demos without BYOK keys. Scope: `eval`.

The test script captures `job_id` for E2/E3.

item.name: E2. Poll job — mdk jobs get

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk jobs get <job_id>`.

Poll until `status` is terminal (`success` / `failed`). On a deployed Azure runtime the worker pod (separate from the API pod) claims the job from the Postgres queue and runs it. For a live stream instead of polling, use `GET /api/v1/jobs/{job_id}/stream` (SSE). When terminal+success, the job carries the eval result id the test script captures into `eval_id` for E3.

exec: // The terminal job exposes the eval result id under one of these.

item.name: E3. Get scorecard — mdk eval show

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk eval show <eval_id>`.

Fetch the persisted scorecard (pass-rate, per-case results, gate verdict) once the job is terminal. The `EvalRecord` lives in Postgres on a deployed runtime — durable across pod restarts.

item.name: Stateful Sessions
item.description:
Server-managed conversation sessions (ADR 045 D10). A session binds a series of agent runs into a persisted conversation history with per-session cost rollup. Create a session first, then pass `session_id` on subsequent `POST /agents/{name}/runs` calls so the runtime threads prior turns as context.

CLI surface: sessions appear in `mdk runs list` (each run shows its `session_id` when one is set).

request.description:
CLI equivalent: sessions surface in `mdk runs list` — no dedicated create verb in the CLI.

Open a new server-managed conversation (ADR 045 D10). Returns a freshly-minted `SessionView` with a new `session_id` (URL-safe hex UUID). The client stores this id and passes it as `session_id` on subsequent `POST /api/v1/agents/{name}/runs` calls — the runtime loads prior turns as context, runs the agent, and appends the new turn.

Sessions are bound to ONE agent (set here; fixed for the session's life — open a new session to target a different agent). Scope: `run`.

Errors: **401** missing/bad bearer token; **403** lacks `run` scope; **422** invalid body (missing `agent`, oversize `title`).

query.description: OPTIONAL — scope to one agent's sessions.
OPTIONAL — cap on returned rows (default 100).

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk runs list` (sessions appear in run listings).

List sessions for the authenticated tenant, ordered `updated_at DESC` (most recently active first). Rollup fields (`turn_count` + `total_*`) are included on each row; the per-turn `messages` history is omitted here — fetch it via `GET /sessions/{id}`. Tenant-scoped — a tenant never sees another tenant's sessions. Scope: `read`.

query.description: OPTIONAL — set false to return only session metadata + rollups without the full message history.

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk runs list` (sessions surface there with turn counts).

Get a session by id with its full conversation history + the per-session cost rollup (ADR 045 D10). When `include_messages=true` (the default) the response includes a `messages` array sorted ASC by `created_at` — earliest turn first. The `{{session_id}}` variable is captured by the `POST /sessions` test script above. Scope: `read`.

Errors: **401** bad token; **404** session doesn't exist OR belongs to a different tenant (same opaque 404 contract as `GET /runs/{id}` — no cross-tenant existence leak).

CLI equivalent: none (API-first — no dedicated CLI delete verb for sessions).

Hard-delete a session and its messages by id. Returns 204 No Content on success. Tenant-scoped — a cross-tenant `session_id` yields 404 (never 403). Scope: `run`.

Errors: **401** missing/bad token; **403** lacks `run` scope; **404** session not found or cross-tenant.

exec: pm.test('204 No Content or 404 if not found', () => [204, 404].includes(pm.response.code));

item.name: POST agents/:name/runs (stateful) — mdk run with session

query.description: Inline synchronous mode — returns RunView (200). Omit for async (202 + job_id).

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk run <agent>` (with `--session <id>` when session support lands in the CLI).

Same as the core-flow run (step 6) but with `session_id` in the body — the runtime loads prior turns as context, executes the agent, and appends the new turn to the session's message history (ADR 045 D10).

Requires a valid `session_id` captured from `POST /sessions`. The session must have been opened for this agent. Scope: `run`.

On each subsequent stateful run the `messages` array in `GET /sessions/{id}` grows by one turn.

item.name: Voice
item.description:
Full-duplex voice for any agent (ADR 048 / ADR 050). Audio flows through STT → the unchanged text Executor → TTS. The pipeline leaves `core/executor.py` untouched — existing agents gain voice with no prompt changes.

CLI equivalent: `mdk voice try <agent>`.

Auth: WebSocket connections authenticate via `?token=<bearer_token>` query param (browsers cannot set Authorization headers on WebSocket handshakes). On auth failure the server closes with 1008 (Policy Violation).

query.description: Bearer token for WebSocket auth (browsers cannot set Authorization headers on WS handshakes).

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk voice try <agent>`.

Full-duplex WebSocket voice for `{agent_name}` (ADR 048 D4, Phase 1 — pipeline mode). Use Postman's WebSocket request type to connect.

**Protocol (pipeline mode, default):** the client streams binary audio frames then sends `{"type":"end"}` to commit a turn. The server replies with:
- `{"type":"transcript.partial", "text":"..."}` — interim STT
- `{"type":"transcript.final", "text":"..."}` — committed transcript
- `{"type":"agent.token", "text":"..."}` — streaming LLM tokens
- binary frames tagged with a small JSON header — TTS audio chunks
- `{"type":"done"}` — turn complete
- `{"type":"error", "message":"..."}` — error (connection stays open)

**Auth:** `?token=<bearer>` query param. Needs `run` scope. Close 1008 on failure.

**Realtime mode:** append `?mode=realtime` to switch to full-duplex voice↔voice through a `RealtimeVoiceProvider` (ADR 048 D2b / Phase 2). See the `?mode=realtime` variant below. A runtime with no realtime factory configured rejects with an `error` frame.

query.description: Bearer token for WebSocket auth.
Realtime full-duplex voice↔voice mode (ADR 048 D2b). Requires a RealtimeVoiceProvider on the runtime.

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk voice try <agent> --mode realtime`.

Realtime full-duplex variant of the same WebSocket endpoint (ADR 048 D2b / Phase 2). The `?mode=realtime` flag switches from the text-Executor pipeline to a `RealtimeVoiceProvider` — voice-NATIVE (no text Executor involved).

**Protocol (realtime mode):** the client streams binary mic frames continuously; the provider's own server-side VAD decides turn boundaries. Server emits:
- `{"type":"speech_started"}` — VAD detected speech
- `{"type":"speech_stopped"}` — VAD silence detected
- `{"type":"transcript.partial"}` / `{"type":"transcript.final"}` — rolling STT
- `{"type":"response_done"}` — full TTS response sent
- binary audio frames — TTS output

**Opt-in / graceful degradation:** a runtime with no realtime factory configured replies with `{"type":"error", "message":"realtime mode not available"}` and closes cleanly. The pipeline path (no `?mode`) is unaffected.

Auth: same `?token=<bearer>` + `run` scope as pipeline mode.

item.name: Graph Analytics
item.description:
Knowledge-graph surface for a project (ADR 046 / ADR 010). Each project maintains a GraphRAG graph; these endpoints expose the graph for rendering (sigma.js / graphology) and entity drill-down.

CLI equivalent: `mdk graph serve --project <id>`.

All endpoints are tenant-scoped — a cross-tenant `project_id` or `node_id` yields an empty document or 404 (no cross-tenant leak). Scope: `read`.

query.description: OPTIONAL — 'knowledge' (GraphRAG, default) or 'topology' (reserved, empty today).
OPTIONAL — center the window on a node (bounded k-hop). Omit for the full-graph overview (still capped).
OPTIONAL — hops from root (capped at 6).
OPTIONAL — node/edge cap (default 500, max 5000).
OPTIONAL — filter to one node type.
OPTIONAL — ADR 046 D1 project-scope filter. Omit for the full per-agent graph (backward-compatible).

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk graph serve --project <id>`.

A windowed subgraph for `project_id` as **graphology JSON** — a sigma.js client feeds this to `graph.import(...)` with zero transform. Each node carries `label` / `type` / degree-derived `size` / `color` (+ `community` when stored); layout `x` / `y` are omitted (the client runs ForceAtlas2). Hard-capped (default 500, max 5000 nodes/edges) to protect the browser.

`project_id` is the agent that owns the graph (one graph per agent). The `project` query param (ADR 046 D1) scopes to a project subgraph within that agent's KB — backward-compatible when omitted. Scope: `read`.

query.description: OPTIONAL — scope lookup to one agent's graph.
OPTIONAL — ADR 046 D1 project-scope filter.

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk graph serve --project <id>` (node panel click).

Detail for one graph node: properties + provenance + neighbors. Returns the node's attributes, its `provenance` (each source chunk's url + snippet + extraction_confidence), the `neighbors` (its 1-hop connected entities — tickets/SOPs/docs — each tagged with relation type + direction, the drill-down panel's clickable list), the live neighbor count, the agents that reference it, and `_links.expand` (the neighbors endpoint).

`{{graph_node_id}}` is captured by the subgraph request test script above. `?project=` scopes to one agent's graph; `?project_id=` (ADR 046 D1, additive) further bounds the node + neighborhood to one project's subgraph. Scope: `read`.

Errors: **401** unauthed; **404** unknown / cross-tenant / cross-project node.

exec: pm.test('200 OK or 404 when graph is empty', () => [200, 404].includes(pm.response.code));

query.description: OPTIONAL — hops to expand (default 1, capped at 6).
OPTIONAL — node/edge cap (default 500, max 5000).
OPTIONAL — ADR 046 D1 project-scope filter.

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk graph serve --project <id>` (click-to-expand interaction).

Expand-on-demand: a node's neighborhood as **graphology JSON**. Drives the click-to-grow interaction — the client imports this document on top of the current graph. Bounded by `depth` (hops, capped at 6) and `limit` (node/edge cap, default 500, max 5000). Unknown / cross-tenant / cross-project node → empty document. Scope: `read`.

query.description: REQUIRED — search query string.
OPTIONAL — scope to one agent's graph.
OPTIONAL — max results (default 20).

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk graph serve --project <id>` (search box).

Semantic search over the knowledge graph. Returns matching nodes ranked by relevance. Use `?project=<agent>` to scope to one agent's graph, or omit to search across all agents in the tenant. Scope: `read`.

item.name: Projects + Catalog
item.description:
Projects (ADR 046 — multi-agent scoping) and Catalog (ADR 041 — agent marketplace). Projects group agents + KBs under a shared `project_id`. The catalog is the curated agent marketplace (browse → fork → deploy).

CLI equivalents: `mdk project create`, `mdk project add-agent`, `mdk catalog list`.

item.name: POST projects — create project — mdk project create

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk project create`.

Create a project in the caller's tenant. `name` is unique within the tenant. The reserved literal `"default"` is rejected (422) — the per-tenant default project is auto-created by storage at first read. `owner_principal_id` defaults to the caller's principal when omitted. Scope: `admin`.

Errors: **401** bad token; **403** no `admin` scope; **409** name collision; **422** name == 'default' (reserved).

query.description: OPTIONAL — surface soft-deleted projects (default false).
OPTIONAL — max rows (default 100, max 500).

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk project list`.

List the caller's tenant's projects, newest-first. Tenant-scoped — a tenant never sees another tenant's projects. Supports pagination via `limit` + `after_id`. Scope: `read`.

item.name: GET projects/:id/agents — list agents in project

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk project list-agents` (not yet a dedicated verb — use `mdk agent list --project <id>`).

Note: the `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/agents` route is the streaming creation-status endpoint (`create-stream/{job_id}`). To list agents in a project, use `GET /api/v1/agents` with a project filter, or `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}` (project detail includes agent references). The streaming endpoint here is for polling long-running agent creation jobs scoped to a project.

Uses `{{run_id}}` from the Core Flow folder as a stand-in for a `job_id`. Scope: `read`.

item.name: POST projects/:id/agents — add agent to project — mdk project add-agent

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk project add-agent`.

Create (or import) an agent scoped to a project — `POST /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/agents` (ADR 046 D1 unified create). Accepts the same discriminated-union body as `POST /api/v1/agents` (JSON wizard submission OR multipart bundle), with project scoping applied automatically.

This is the NLP-based "describe the agent in natural language" path when the body carries a `description` field (the runtime's LLM authoring stage converts it to a wizard submission). All legacy create paths continue to work byte-for-byte — this endpoint composes them. Scope: `admin`.

Errors: **401** bad token; **403** no `admin` scope; **404** project not found or cross-tenant; **422** malformed body.

item.name: GET catalog/agents — browse catalog — mdk catalog list

query.description: OPTIONAL — narrow to one namespace: 'movate', 'private', 'community'.
OPTIONAL — single-tag membership match.
OPTIONAL — exact ADR 028 shape taxonomy match.
OPTIONAL — case-insensitive substring over name / title / description.
OPTIONAL — page size (default 100, max 500).

request.description:
CLI equivalent: `mdk catalog list`.

List catalog entries visible to the caller (ADR 041 D2/D5). Visibility join: all `movate` entries + the caller's `private` entries + (future) `community`. Filters (all ANDed): `source`, `tag`, `shape`, `q`. Pagination via `limit` + `after_slug`.

The catalog is the curated agent marketplace — browse here to find pre-built agents you can fork and deploy into your own project. Scope: `read`.
